# Purified Roblox ARM64 Linux Runtime Roadmap

## Goal

Build a native ARM64 Linux runtime that can host the Roblox Android APK payload on Orange Pi 5 Plus class hardware, with a long-term path to Mesa Vulkan acceleration. The runtime is intentionally not Flatpak-based. It should run from a normal project directory, prefer Wayland, and keep all experiments sandbox-safe.

## Current Artifact Facts

- Local package root: `roblox_2.718.1110-an1.com/assets/app`
- Split APKs present: `base.apk`, `config.arm64_v8a.apk`, `config.armeabi_v7a.apk`
- ARM64 native payload found in `config.arm64_v8a.apk`
- `scripts/roblox-login-readiness-report.sh .` currently reports `score:11/11` for login-adjacent local artifacts: `WebLoginProtocol`, `PlayerApp.Login`, `onDataModelNotificationLogin`, WebView protocol exports, Android `WebView` in `classes.dex`, app surface JNI, Android `Surface`, EGL window/swap symbols, Android Vulkan surface symbol, MessageBus, and CookieManager are all present in this APK/lib pair.
- Key bridge targets:
  - `lib/arm64-v8a/libroblox.so`
  - `lib/arm64-v8a/libloader.so`
  - `lib/arm64-v8a/libtrampoline.so`
- The top-level `AndroidManifest.xml` in `roblox_2.718.1110-an1.com` is from an installer wrapper. Runtime work should target the nested `assets/app/base.apk` and architecture split, not the wrapper.

## Milestone 1: Native Window Host

Status: implemented as prototype.

Deliverables:

- C/CMake native host binary: `purified-host`
- SDL2 windowing backend with Wayland preference and X11/dummy fallback
- Rendering abstraction that draws a deterministic host surface
- Host `PurifiedAndroidSurface` model with safe Java-surface handle mapping, size tracking, refcounting, frame serials, and an ARGB8888 pixel buffer
- APK bridge scanner for Roblox split APK payloads
- Vulkan loader/device probe
- Smoke tests through CTest
- Launcher scripts for build, test, run, and optional ARM64 library extraction

Acceptance:

- `./scripts/test.sh` builds and runs APK bridge tests.
- `./scripts/launch.sh` opens a Linux window on Orange Pi 5 Plus.
- `VIDEO=wayland ./scripts/launch.sh` uses Wayland where the compositor supports it.
- `./build/purified-host --video dummy --frames 3` runs headless for CI/shell validation.
- By default, `./scripts/launch.sh` now runs the strict raw native lifecycle sequence before opening the host window. Set `RUN_NATIVE_SEQUENCE=0` to launch only the host placeholder.
- Set `NATIVE_SEQUENCE_MODE=render` to run the deeper 18-step render-adjacent Roblox native sequence before opening the host window.

## Architecture

### Host Process

`purified-host` owns the native Linux process, window lifecycle, event pump, and bridge lifecycle. The current host uses SDL2 because it provides a stable Wayland/X11/dummy selection layer without forcing early protocol boilerplate into the runtime.

Long-term, the SDL layer can remain as a bring-up backend while a direct Wayland backend is added for tighter control over `xdg-shell`, pointer lock, relative pointer, fractional scaling, and Vulkan surface creation.

### Rendering Layer

The current renderer is SDL-backed and intentionally simple. It proves the host surface and frame loop. It now renders from the host-owned `PurifiedAndroidSurface` pixel buffer instead of drawing all placeholder geometry directly through SDL, so the visible Linux window follows the same lock/write/present shape needed for Android surface bridging.

The abstraction boundary is:

- window setup: `src/window_sdl.c`
- renderer API: `include/purified/render.h`
- current renderer: `src/render_sdl.c`

The WebView/login boundary is now a first-class host object:

- WebView host API: `include/purified/webview_host.h`
- WebView host state model: `src/webview_host.c`
- WebView surface API: `include/purified/webview_surface.h`
- WebView surface model: `src/webview_surface.c`
- Focused bridge test: `tests/webview_host_test.c`
- Focused WebView surface test: `tests/webview_surface_test.c`

This object converts the native render-pump report into a stable host contract: WebView ready, login route ready, login-window request ready, open-window command, command payload, visible flag, URL/domain/user-agent, MessageBus method, cookie readiness, app-shell readiness, and asset readiness. The current WebView surface turns that host contract into a separate Cairo/Pango-rendered ARGB buffer that the SDL renderer composites over the Android surface. It now handles pointer focus, text input, password masking, Tab, Backspace, Enter, and submit-attempt state locally. A future embedded WebView or Android UI surface can consume the same host object without rewriting the native probe.

When `webkit2gtk-4.0` is available, the build also creates `purified-webkit-login`. The SDL host can start it from the modeled Roblox WebView `openWindow` state with `PURIFIED_WEBVIEW_BACKEND=webkit`. This currently opens a separate GTK/WebKit process for `https://www.roblox.com/login`, keeping GTK's event loop isolated from SDL while proving the real web-login window path.

Next rendering target is a Vulkan renderer that creates a native Wayland or X11 surface, selects the Mesa Vulkan device, and renders swapchain frames. After that, Android graphics interop work can target Vulkan/ANativeWindow-compatible paths.

### APK Bridge

The current bridge is a scanner/validator, not an Android userspace implementation. It reads the split APK central directory, validates required ARM64 `.so` files, records their sizes, and exposes bridge readiness state to the host.

Next bridge layers:

1. Extract native libraries to a controlled build/runtime directory.
2. Inspect ELF dynamic dependencies and JNI exports.
3. Build a shim loader that can `dlopen` controlled test libraries first.
4. Add Android bionic/linker compatibility strategy.
5. Add binder/property/asset/input/surface stubs only as demanded by observed failures.

## Android Compatibility Plan

Roblox Android native code expects more than glibc and a desktop window. The compatibility layer must be incremental and measured.

Near-term experiments:

- Use `scripts/extract-apk-libs.sh` to extract ARM64 libraries into `build/apk-libs`.
- Run `readelf -d` on `libloader.so`, `libtrampoline.so`, and `libroblox.so`.
- Record `NEEDED` entries and unresolved Android-specific dependencies.
- Identify JNI entrypoints with `readelf -Ws`.
- Create a small loader experiment that verifies ordinary glibc `dlopen` failure modes without patching system paths.

Expected compatibility surfaces:

- bionic libc/linker assumptions
- Android system properties
- AAssetManager and APK asset lookup
- JNI VM lifecycle
- ANativeWindow surface lifecycle
- ALooper, input queue, and sensors
- OpenSL/AAudio or audio stubs
- network and TLS behavior
- filesystem layout for app data/cache

Observed initial ELF dependencies:

- `libloader.so`: `libdl.so`, `libz.so`, `libm.so`, `libandroid.so`, `libc.so`
- `libtrampoline.so`: `liblog.so`, `libdl.so`, `libc.so`
- `libroblox.so`: `libandroid.so`, `libm.so`, `libOpenSLES.so`, `libOpenMAXAL.so`, `libmediandk.so`, `libGLESv2.so`, `libEGL.so`, `liblog.so`, `libdl.so`, `libc.so`

Observed direct `dlopen` failures on the ARM64 Linux host:

- `libloader.so`: cannot resolve Android-named `libdl.so`
- `libtrampoline.so`: marked/loadable as a position-independent executable rather than a normal shared object
- `libroblox.so`: cannot resolve `libandroid.so`

Observed after local Android shim and patched undefined symbol versions:

- `libroblox.so` reaches ELF initializer execution under glibc and then segfaults at address `0x0` from `_dl_init`.
- The crash is consistent with Android packed relocation records being present but not applied by glibc.
- `libroblox.so` contains Android-specific dynamic relocation tags `DT_ANDROID_RELA` / `DT_ANDROID_RELASZ`.
- This makes plain glibc `dlopen` a dead end for full execution; the next runtime milestone needs either a bionic/linker64 environment or a custom loader that can apply Android packed relocations before constructors run.
- Custom loader probe now maps `libroblox.so`, decodes Android APS2 packed relocations, applies `R_AARCH64_RELATIVE`, and verifies the init array is no longer null before constructor execution.
- Custom loader probe also applies standard PLT relocations for `libroblox.so`.
- Current loader probe result after signed packed-offset fix: `android_rela_count=562881`, `relative_applied=562796`, `plt_resolved=621`, `plt_unresolved=0`.
- Mesa `libEGL.so.1` and `libGLESv2.so.2` are loaded globally for import resolution, giving the native Roblox binary a Linux GL/GLES path for the next constructor-execution experiment.

Next constructor execution gate:

- Set mapped segment permissions from `PT_LOAD` flags after relocation. Done.
- Execute init functions in a crash-contained child process. Done.
- Record first Android API call or fault from constructor execution. Current result: constructors 1-3 return, constructor 4 faults with `SIGSEGV` at `libroblox.so` virtual address `0x1f1fadc`.
- Replace broad stubs with stateful runtime objects where constructors require real behavior.

Constructor probe command:

```sh
MAX_CTORS=16 ./scripts/android-constructor-probe.sh
```

Observed output:

```text
constructors_attempted=4 child_exit_code=0 child_signal=11
last_constructor_vaddr=0x1f1fadc
```

After fixing signed Android packed relocation offsets, constructor 4 no longer null-dereferences. It reaches an internal allocator abort at `abort_caller_vaddr=0x29b4720`. The guarded constructor scanner can intercept aborts/signals and continue through selected windows.

Observed range scan samples:

```text
START_CTOR=0   MAX_CTORS=12  -> constructors_attempted=12, aborts_skipped=1, signals_skipped=5

START_CTOR=3072 MAX_CTORS=32 -> constructors_attempted=3104, aborts_skipped=1, signals_skipped=0
```

Known hard windows:

- `START_CTOR=12` and `START_CTOR=13` time out in the current probe.
- A sampled window around `START_CTOR=2048` also timed out.
- These are now runtime behavior problems rather than loader/linker failures.

Constructor classifier milestone:

- Added `scripts/constructor-classify.sh`, which builds once and runs each constructor in its own guarded process with an outer timeout.
- This avoids one stateful initializer wedging the entire scan.
- The earlier timeout windows are sequence-dependent: individual constructors in those ranges can execute when isolated.
- Added `scripts/constructor-fast-scan.sh`, backed by a native fork-per-constructor scanner. It maps and relocates `libroblox.so` once, protects segments, then forks one crash-contained child per init function from the relocated image.
- The fast scanner removes the shell/process-launch cost from broad constructor classification and preserves a clean copy-on-write image for each constructor.

Current isolated classification samples:

```text
build/ctor-scan-0-127.tsv: total=128 ok=128 aborting=47 signaling=1 timeouts=0
build/ctor-scan-128-255.tsv: total=128 ok=128 aborting=68 signaling=0 timeouts=0
build/ctor-scan-2048-2111.tsv: total=64 ok=64 aborting=42 signaling=0 timeouts=0
build/ctor-fast-scan-0-1023.tsv: total=1024 ok=671 abort=349 signal=4 timeout=0 crash=0
```

```text
index=5 vaddr=0x1f1f488 fault_vaddr=0x21dccfc
```

The dominant remaining blocker is no longer relocation/import resolution. It is runtime semantics inside constructors: allocator/runtime aborts and stateful constructor interactions.

Native JNI entrypoint milestone:

- Added `scripts/jni-onload-probe.sh` and `--jni-onload` support in `android-loader-probe`.
- `JNI_OnLoad` is exported at virtual address `0x21dd6e8`.
- The first instructions are not normal JVM setup. They issue inline ARM64 syscalls (`socket`, `connect`, `read`) and branch to the 8-byte target expected from that gate.
- On this host, the gate read does not provide a target, so the unmodified entry branches to non-code such as `0x74000001`.
- The probe now patches the mapped in-memory copy at `JNI_OnLoad+0xa4` with `adr x3, .+44`, preserving the wrapper stack flow while routing execution to the real JNI body at `JNI_OnLoad+0xd0`. This patch is local to the anonymous mapped image and does not modify the APK or extracted `.so` on disk.
- Added a fake JavaVM/JNIEnv table and an ARM64 seeded-stack trampoline so the entry gate has the stack slots and preserved registers the Roblox body expects.
- Added typed JNI responses for the early slots Roblox uses: `FindClass`, `NewGlobalRef`, `GetMethodID`, `GetFieldID`, `GetStaticMethodID`, `RegisterNatives`, and `ExceptionCheck`.
- Corrected the fake JNIEnv slot layout against a real ARM64 `jni.h`. The bridge now handles the early object/static call slots, proper instance long slots (`52..54`), proper instance void slots (`61..63`), object/primitive field reads, string UTF extraction/release, and visible `CallVoidMethod(..., run)` telemetry for future Java `Runnable` delivery.
- Added compact JNI tracing with class, method, signature, and fallback stub return addresses.
- With the gate bypassed and fake JNI installed, Roblox reaches the native `JNI_OnLoad` body, calls `GetEnv`, calls through JNI table entries, and emits real Android log lines:
  - `TelemetryProtocol::setProcessTimeOverride: 0`
- The bridge now gets through `NativeSettingsInterface_nativeSetDeviceInfo`: Roblox calls `getDeviceStaticParams`, reads fields including `osVersion`, `deviceName`, `appVersion`, `manufacturer`, `deviceSku`, `appBuildVariant`, `cpu64Bit`, and `socModel`, and converts string fields through `GetStringUTFChars`/`ReleaseStringUTFChars`.
- Added JNI-phase failure accounting (`jni_aborts_skipped`, `jni_signals_skipped`) so constructor failures and `JNI_OnLoad` failures are no longer conflated in probe output.
- Added scoped in-memory compatibility patches for the current probe harness:
  - Redirect Roblox's internal small allocation/free entrypoints used by this startup path to host libc allocation.
  - Return null from an uninitialized Android metadata lookup table instead of dereferencing a missing runtime table.
  - Stub `pthread_create`/`pthread_join` so Android-oriented startup worker threads are not launched before the bridge owns lifecycle state.
  - Patch one Android thread-helper stack-guard exit path that is not meaningful in the synthetic host process.
- These patches are applied only to the anonymous mapped image in the probe process. The APK and extracted `libroblox.so` stay untouched on disk.
- This moved the major `JNI_OnLoad` boundary past the old allocator abort at `abort_caller_vaddr=0x29b4720`, past the follow-on metadata fault and helper stack-guard abort, and through the broad NativeGL/audio/video registration sequence.
- Current major milestone: the probe executes the real Roblox native `JNI_OnLoad` registration path and returns `JNI_VERSION_1_6` (`0x10006`) through an explicit probe completion bridge after the final safe class lookup.
- The latest trace reaches 111 typed JNI calls before the completion bridge. It includes:
  - `NativeGLJavaInterface` device parameter reads and NativeGL method registration.
  - `SessionReporterJavaInterface` setup and session-report method lookup.
  - FMOD/audio/video classes including `org/fmod/AudioDevice`, `org/fmod/MediaCodec`, and `org/fmod/FMOD`.
  - `MediaCodecInfoUtils` codec capability queries.
  - `NetworkUtils`, memstorage/messagebus connection classes, and `NativeTextBoxInfo`.
- A deeper WebRTC Java class-cache helper can be reached with the Android process tail enabled; it currently depends on real JVM/global-reference semantics. The probe defers that helper until the bridge owns Java class lifetime and Android lifecycle state.
- The remaining boundary is no longer "can the Roblox binary execute?" for `JNI_OnLoad`; it does. The next boundary is driving the registered Roblox Android lifecycle/native surface entrypoints with credible fake Activity, asset, filesystem, and ANativeWindow state.
- Added `--jni-onload-after-ctors [count]`, which runs a guarded constructor prefix in the same mapped child before calling `JNI_OnLoad`. Safe prefixes such as 8 constructors preserve JNI progress; larger sequential prefixes still expose constructor-order hangs and need a real constructor dependency plan.

Current JNI probe command:

```sh
./scripts/jni-onload-probe.sh
AFTER_CTORS=8 ./scripts/jni-onload-probe.sh
./scripts/native-entry-probe.sh Java_com_roblox_engine_jni_NativeGLInterface_nativeInitClientSettings
```

Native lifecycle entrypoint milestone:

- Added `--native-entry symbol` support to `android-loader-probe` and `scripts/native-entry-probe.sh`.
- The native-entry harness maps and relocates `libroblox.so`, applies the same probe-local runtime patches, creates a fake JavaVM/JNIEnv, creates fake Java string handles with real UTF contents, and calls selected exported `Java_com_roblox_*` entrypoints in a crash-contained child.
- Added deterministic probe stubs for Android-oriented pthread/TLS/condition/once imports so lifecycle probes do not block on glibc synchronization semantics.
- Added a host-backed allocator singleton for the native startup path at the observed global allocator slot used by vector/string growth.
- Added Android `__errno` compatibility so native startup code no longer crashes when it expects bionic's `__errno` export.
- Confirmed native filesystem/settings entrypoints execute real Roblox code and return:
  - `NativeSettingsInterface_nativeSetBaseDataDirectories`: converts two Java strings through JNI and returns.
  - `NativeGLInterface_nativeInitClientSettings`: converts three Java strings through JNI and returns `0x1`.
- Added `scripts/native-lifecycle-sweep.sh`, which runs the main exported lifecycle/start/surface JNI entrypoints in one guarded pass and writes per-symbol logs under `build/native-lifecycle/`.
- Added `scripts/native-sequence-probe.sh` and `--native-sequence`, which maps/relocates `libroblox.so` once, keeps one fake JavaVM/JNIEnv and one set of Roblox globals alive, and calls the main startup sequence in one child:
  1. `NativeSettingsInterface_nativeSetBaseDataDirectories`
  2. `NativeGLInterface_nativeInitClientSettings`
  3. `NativeGLInterface_nativeGameGlobalInit`
  4. `NativeGLInterface_nativeAppBridgeV2StartAppWithParams`
  5. `NativeGLInterface_nativeAppBridgeV2UpdateSurfaceAppWithPlatformParams`
- Added probe-local lifecycle compatibility for:
  - adjacent platform/config small-string globals used by app/global startup;
  - a synthetic Android log gate plus a no-op internal variadic log helper, avoiding `vfprintf` crashes from incomplete Android logging state;
  - primitive JNI field getters including `GetFloatField` for `PlatformParams.dpiScale`;
  - a fake `ANativeWindow_fromSurface` object in the Android shim;
  - a minimal host-backed bridge singleton object used by the V2 app/surface path.
  - no-op handling for probe-only diagnostic/exception/call-protocol helpers that otherwise consume synthetic bridge state as function pointers or exception objects.
  - a probe allocator/free shim that over-allocates and only frees owned pointers inside the short-lived child, protecting host glibc from synthetic Roblox bridge pointers.
  - a host bridge latch/dispatch experiment for the V2 app bridge callback wrapper.
- `NativeGLInterface_nativeGameGlobalInit` now advances past the previous pthread timeout, string allocator fault, missing `__errno`, missing allocator singleton, and platform/config string accessor. It returns cleanly in the native-entry harness.
- `NativeAppBridgeInterface_nativeAppBridgeAppStart...` now advances past the old platform/config boundary and reaches a later app-start null runtime state:

```text
fault_vaddr=0x22dd774
fault_lr_vaddr=0x22dd550
```

- `NativeGLInterface_nativeAppBridgeV2StartAppWithParams` and `NativeGLInterface_nativeAppBridgeV2UpdateSurfaceAppWithPlatformParams` now execute real Roblox JNI object extraction for `surface`, `platformParams`, `vrContext`, `username`, and `dpiScale`. Their current shared boundary is a bridge-object virtual dispatch:

```text
fault_vaddr=0x29988d4
fault_lr_vaddr=0x29988c0
```

This is a later and more useful boundary than JNI bootstrap: Roblox is executing exported app/lifecycle/native-surface methods and assembling app-start platform parameters. The next bridge work is to replace the synthetic bridge object with a credible NativeGL/app bridge object graph, then connect that to a real host `ANativeWindow`/Wayland/Vulkan surface.

Stateful sequence milestone:

```text
./scripts/native-sequence-probe.sh
constructors_attempted=5
last_constructor_vaddr=0x25824e8
jni_stub_calls=30
```

The runtime now drives a single mapped Roblox native image through base directories, client settings, global init, V2 app start, and V2 surface update. This is now a clean stateful sequence: the child exits with code 0, no child signal, and no recoverable signal skips. The important change was replacing the fake-success `pthread_create` shim with real Linux pthread creation, which lets Roblox startup worker routines actually run instead of deadlocking the app-start path.

Latest native-start compatibility added:

- Host-backed short-string initializer for null Android config strings.
- Null-safe accessors for optional logging/provider/config services.
- Probe-local completion of a one-shot engine init flag that otherwise spun forever in synthetic Android state.
- Host-backed `ANativeWindow_fromSurface`/acquire/release/size hooks that map synthetic Java `Surface` handles onto a probe-local native-window wrapper and default Android surface object.
- The native sequence harness now calls the five lifecycle entries with entry-specific argument shapes. `nativeAppBridgeV2StartAppWithParams` receives the APK-declared `com/roblox/engine/jni/autovalue/StartAppParams` object, and `nativeAppBridgeV2UpdateSurfaceAppWithPlatformParams` receives separate `Surface` and `PlatformParams` objects instead of placeholder strings.
- `scripts/native-sequence-raw-surface.sh` disables the broad surface/context bypass and now executes the full five-step native sequence without child signals or guarded skip recovery.
- Raw surface-context mode routes Roblox's synthetic worker-drain body (`0x2251768`) and condition-wait helper (`0x22a8128`) into host task-queue shims. These replace the old invisible returns with observable counters while still bounding the missing Android app queue state.
- Signal-time register capture in `PurifiedAndroidLoadReport`, useful when the probe crosses from mapped Roblox code into host libc or pthreads.

The prototype post-settings profile now bounds the observed post-client-settings blockers:

- `0x22aaa70`: skips the incomplete config/settings owner helper that wrote through a bogus owner pointer and previously faulted at `0x22aab54`.
- `0x22abe3c` / `0x22abea0`: routes the owner-helper canary failure back through the normal epilogue instead of `__stack_chk_fail` / `0x22abea4`.
- `0x6202388`: returns false from the setting-string predicate that was scanning a sentinel-backed fake setting.
- `0x22ac0d0` / `0x22ac140`: routes the nested canary failure past the later `0x22ac144` abort.
- `0x2654ffc` / `0x2654bd4`: no-ops two task-enqueue helpers that dereference an uninitialized Android/native scheduler singleton.
- `0x22a9908`: skips a post-settings flag-provider construction path that reached the old optional-cast fault at `0x296a0ec`.
- `0x22a993c`: skips cleanup of a poison sentinel after that construction path is bypassed.
- `0x22ad5f0`: returns from a post-settings service lookup helper whose backing Android/native service singleton is not modeled yet.
- `0x22ae628` / `0x22ae62c` / `0x22ae630`: bypasses a cold stack/log failure island after settings parsing.
- `0x22ae910`: redirects a cleanup backedge to the same-frame epilogue instead of re-entering a torn-down settings frame.
- `0x22b6770` / `0x624f53c` / `0x26568c0`: bounds crashpad/logging and unsupported-GL probes whose Android backends are not present on Linux yet.
- `0x22b88a0` / `0x22b8da8` / `0x22bd9c8` / `0x22bdbfc` / `0x29e6d94` / `0x22caa90`: returns from lifecycle/config/event singleton tails that otherwise dereference missing app services.
- `0x22b62f4` / `0x22b62f8` / `0x22b6318`: bypasses the generated protocol callback tail that previously reached the bridge-dispatch finalizer fault at `0x2998d14`.
- `0x223e37c` / `0x223f08c` / `0x22b63e0`: bounds telemetry event/log helpers and their cleanup tail.
- `0x226c010` and `0x226c898`: bound client-settings numeric scratch appends that can write through an uninitialized synthetic settings parser buffer. The probe feeds static settings strings, so the scratch copies are not needed for the current host model and removing them eliminated intermittent `nativeInitClientSettings` signal skips.

With those prototype patches, step 9 now advances past the original settings-owner/canary/string/task blockers, the old `0x296a0ec` Java flag-provider optional-cast fault, and the old `0x2998d14` bridge-dispatch/finalizer signal boundary. The current post-settings report is `constructors_attempted=9`, `child_signal=0`, and `nativePostClientSettingsLoadedInitialization3:ok`. This profile is installed late, immediately before the ninth post-settings call, because enabling it before `nativeInitClientSettings` can perturb the stricter client-settings path; the next correctness milestone is replacing the bypasses with shaped fake Android settings/provider, lifecycle, telemetry, and app-service objects.

These replace the missing `.bss` gate prologues with save frames that match each export's epilogue, keep the game-start JNI parameter extraction path live, and bound AppReady as a no-op notification while the real host app-event bridge is still absent.

It indicates the next render blockers are no longer Java method discovery or entry-frame shape; they are real host app-event dispatch, Android bridge object ownership, and replacing the synthetic ANativeWindow with a surface/buffer path that Roblox can render into.

Roblox obtains a host `ANativeWindow`, calls into the interposed EGL loader path, creates an EGL display/config/context/window surface, makes the context current, queries the surface, resolves GL entrypoints with `eglGetProcAddress`, reaches `eglSwapBuffers` once, and then executes Roblox code that calls `glBindFramebuffer`, `glGenFramebuffers`, `glCheckFramebufferStatus`, `glFramebufferTexture2D`, `glFramebufferRenderbuffer`, `glDeleteFramebuffers`, `glViewport`, `glScissor`, `glUseProgram`, `glCreateShader`, `glShaderSource`, `glCompileShader`, `glGetShaderiv`, `glGetShaderInfoLog`, `glCreateProgram`, `glAttachShader`, `glBindAttribLocation`, `glLinkProgram`, `glGetProgramiv`, `glGetProgramInfoLog`, `glGetIntegerv`, `glGetUniformLocation`, `glUniform1i`, `glReleaseShaderCompiler`, `glGenBuffers`, `glBufferData`, `glBufferSubData`, `glActiveTexture`, `glGenTextures`, `glBindTexture`, `glTexImage2D`, `glPixelStorei`, `glTexParameteri`, `glTexParameterf`, `glTexParameterfv`, `glTexSubImage2D`, `glCopyTexSubImage2D`, `glCompressedTexImage2D`, `glCompressedTexSubImage2D`, `glGenerateMipmap`, `glReadPixels`, `glEnable`, `glDisable`, `glCullFace`, `glPolygonOffset`, `glBlendFunc`, `glBlendFuncSeparate`, `glDepthFunc`, `glBindBuffer`, `glEnableVertexAttribArray`, `glVertexAttribPointer`, `glDrawArrays`, `glDrawElements`, `glColorMask`, `glDepthMask`, `glStencilMask`, `glClearColor`, `glClearDepthf`, `glClearStencil`, `glClear`, and `glGetError`. The GLES interposer now turns Roblox `glClear`, `glDrawArrays`, and `glDrawElements` activity into deterministic writes on the shared Android surface buffer, while `eglSwapBuffers` only marks/report-presents that surface. The SDL launcher preserves that buffer once the native sequence has presented. It also crosses native login-readiness calls for Lua login and WebLogin cold-start/init modeling, initializes the modeled WebView protocol, models WebView user-agent/window/callback/domain surfaces, records a concrete WebView `openWindow` request for `https://www.roblox.com/login`, creates JNI strings for WebView and magic-login/account protocol keys, crosses URL-linking/BaseUrl protocol setup, covers additional account/linking key families used by integrity and deep-link login routing, and now reaches host-modeled MessageBus raw publish/request/telemetry, cookie get/set/conversion, logging, telemetry, call, audio, and platform callback boundaries. The host queue records typed categories for first-frame, app-event, lifecycle, startup, WebView, and MessageBus work, with sign-in-shaped WebView, messagebus, cookie, and logging payload state instead of only counters. It has not yet produced visible Roblox UI or full Roblox-authored frame submission in the Linux window, but the client is now past Java/JNI registration, past exported app/surface startup, past foreground/startup/environment handoff, into native renderer setup, through a present boundary that produces a host-presentable surface buffer, into real Roblox GLES framebuffer creation/status/attachment/delete, capability query, viewport, clear, shader compile/source/status/info-log, shader program link/status/info-log, shader compiler release, program, sampler uniform, buffer upload/subdata, texture upload, texture parameter state, texture subimage, compressed texture image, compressed texture subimage update, mipmap generation, framebuffer-to-texture copy, texture readback, render-state apply including separate RGB/alpha blending, vertex attribute, and indexed/non-indexed draw paths, and through all exported Java/JNI bridge calls available in this APK build without guarded recovery.
- EGL/GLES contract hardening now tracks `eglGetCurrentDisplay` alongside current context/draw/read surfaces, and the real-GLES backend forwards Mesa `glGetIntegerv`, `glGetString`, and `glRenderbufferStorageMultisample` when a surfaceless Mesa context is available. The fallback values remain in place for hosts without working EGL/GLES, which keeps the prototype portable while making Orange Pi/Panfrost runs more honest.
- Host presentation now gives native GLES readback content the primary SDL window surface when available. Diagnostic framing/status rails remain for placeholder frames, but real native content is copied directly to the full viewport and screenshots capture that native surface path.
- Surface-buffer bridge milestone:
  - `PurifiedAndroidSurface` now owns a resizable ARGB8888 pixel buffer, stride, pixel format, and buffer serial.
  - The surface tracks configure/lock/unlock counts so host and probe reports can distinguish "surface exists" from "surface is being used as a buffer target."
  - The SDL window loop locks and writes the host surface buffer each frame, and the renderer presents that buffer through a streaming SDL texture.
  - The Android shim exports `ANativeWindow_setBuffersGeometry`, `ANativeWindow_lock`, and `ANativeWindow_unlockAndPost` in addition to the existing acquire/release/fromSurface/size calls.
  - The Android shim also exports `ANativeWindow_getFormat`, and `PurifiedAndroidSurface` exposes the current pixel format so APK sidecars can query ARGB8888 surface metadata through the normal Android native-window API.
  - A minimal `libjnigraphics.so` shim now supports `AndroidBitmap_getInfo`, `AndroidBitmap_lockPixels`, and `AndroidBitmap_unlockPixels` over the same host surface buffer. This lets graphics/image sidecars inspect or lock bridge-owned bitmap/surface memory instead of failing at import time.
  - `libsurface_util_jni.so`, `librenderscript-toolkit.so`, and `libimage_processing_util_jni.so` now directly `dlopen` under the local Android shim. The libc compatibility layer was expanded with many Android `LIBC`-versioned process, pthread, stdio, socket, time, locale, signal, xattr, mmap/mremap/msync, epoll/eventfd/timerfd, network, process-identity, stdio, and string/wide-string forwarders.
  - The APK-wide non-executing Android libc ABI check is now clean: every local APK native-library `@LIBC` import is provided by the local shim set. Directly loading large Android libraries with host `dlopen` has moved beyond unresolved imports into constructor/runtime behavior: `libroblox.so` now reaches a runtime segfault when loaded as a plain host shared object, while the actual custom loader path remains the supported execution path; `libtrampoline.so` remains a PIE executable and is not expected to `dlopen` like a shared object.
  - The direct native-loader resolver exposes the same `ANativeWindow` buffer functions for in-process probes if a future Roblox import or experiment requests them.
  - The native loader report now records `window_create`, `set_geometry`, `lock`, and `unlock_post` counters. The current render sequence reaches `window_create:5`, proving Roblox asks the bridge for native window wrappers during app/game surface setup.
- The loader interposes Roblox's EGL/GLES imports with probe-local counters and safe fake handles. Current 18-step render sequence reports `window_create:5`, `host_app_events=dispatch:11`, and `host_queue=enqueued:11 pending:11`. It now returns cleanly with zero signal/abort recovery, doubled EGL setup, `gl_calls=318`, `get_iv:11`, and `530:smoke:ok`. The key fix was making GL strings and integer limits look like an Android GLES 3.x device instead of returning one placeholder value for every query.
  - This is still host-generated pixels, not Roblox-rendered pixels, but it changes the prototype from "window draws a placeholder" to "window presents a bridge-owned Android surface buffer."
- `purified-host` can now run the same raw surface-context native lifecycle sequence from the normal launcher path with:

```sh
./build/purified-host --apk-root roblox_2.718.1110-an1.com/assets/app --video dummy --frames 1 --run-native-sequence --raw-surface-sequence --native-sequence-lib build/apk-libs/lib/arm64-v8a/libroblox.so
```


- Diagnostic mode `PURIFIED_SEED_MAIN_THREAD=1` seeds the observed Roblox main-thread id global (`0x6802570`) to the host pthread shim's current-thread value. Using that while removing the worker-drain/condition-wait patches exposed the next real app-bridge state bug: the direct dispatch path reaches `fault_vaddr=0x2a169c4` from `fault_lr_vaddr=0x22514bc`, a vector/queue write with an uninitialized destination. The strict default path keeps the narrower worker/wait patches until that queue object is modeled.

Current boundary:

- The native Roblox binary is executing well past Java/JNI registration and through the exported app/surface startup sequence, including raw surface-context mode without guarded skip recovery.
- The normal launcher can now execute the strict five-step native sequence and then present the Linux host window in a ready state. The extended eight-step sequence is available as an explicit probe and is now covered by CTest.
- The automatic post-settings prototype profile now completes the ninth native initializer cleanly, and the render launcher reaches the full 18-entry render-adjacent native sequence with zero signal/abort recovery on the stricter render patch set.
- `./launch.sh` now defaults to `NATIVE_SEQUENCE_MODE=app-pump` plus `PURIFIED_APP_PUMP_STEADY_FRAMES=1`, so the normal user launch path opens the SDL host window after the deeper live-app native sequence that avoids logout/pause/destroy teardown, crosses the legacy AppShell/LuaApp `nativeAppBridgeAppStart` export as a real Roblox call, repeatedly drains the main-thread queue, and posts 33 Android surface frames through Roblox's EGL present-tail probe. Environment overrides still work for `basic`, `extended`, `render`, `render-pump`, and `app-pump`; set `PURIFIED_APP_PUMP_STEADY_FRAMES=0` to return to the shorter 334-call app-pump diagnostic. The older `render-pump` mode remains the strict export-coverage path.
- `NATIVE_SEQUENCE_MODE=app-pump` reaches a 334-entry native app pump with zero signal/abort recovery, fifteen host native-window wrapper creations, twenty-two host app-event dispatches, foreground lifecycle, real cache/files/preferences/version settings handoff, legacy Lua-app startup, the real `NativeAppBridgeInterface.nativeAppBridgeAppStart(...)` boundary, MainGameActivity startup hooks, APK asset-manager readiness, graphics-readiness/device-display/GameActivity handoff, Android display-refresh/supported-refresh/Java-heap environment handoff, four first-frame schedules, four deliverable fake-Java `Runnable` callback objects, fifteen clean main-thread queue drains, a clean direct call into Roblox's EGL renderer bootstrap, nine Roblox present-tail `eglSwapBuffers` calls, and safe internal Roblox GLES helpers that now create/link a mapped Mesa shader/program from separate vertex and fragment shader helpers, upload a real Roblox buffer with `glBufferSubData`, bind it as the app-pump array/element stream, run framebuffer attachment/build/status, renderbuffer storage/multisample storage, texture parameter state, compressed texture image/subimage, mipmap, framebuffer-to-texture copy, render-state, blend-separate, stencil-state, and draw through Mesa without bridge-synthetic shader, vertex, or index fallback. The fake JNI handle arena now has enough slots for the full protocol-heavy app-pump run, so late `PlatformParams.dpiScale` lookups stay typed as `com/roblox/engine/jni/model/PlatformParams` instead of aliasing back to the probe class. The current short live-app diagnostic reports `host_queue_callbacks=records:4 delivered:4 first_frame:4 class:java/lang/Runnable`, `gl_calls=257`, `gl_create_shader:2`, `gl_shader_source:2`, `gl_compile_shader:2`, `gl_create_program:1`, `gl_attach_shader:2`, `gl_bind_attrib_location:1`, `gl_link_program:1`, `gl_use_program:3`, `gl_draw_arrays:2`, `gl_draw_elements:1`, `gl_bind_buffer:5`, `gl_buffer_data:1`, `gl_buffer_sub_data:1`, `gl_active_texture:8`, `gl_gen_textures:2`, `gl_bind_texture:15`, `gl_tex_parameteri:5`, `gl_tex_parameterf:2`, `gl_tex_parameterfv:1`, `gl_tex_image_2d:2`, `gl_tex_sub_image_2d:1`, `gl_compressed_tex_image_2d:1`, `gl_compressed_tex_sub_image_2d:1`, `gl_generate_mipmap:1`, `gl_copy_tex_sub_image_2d:1`, and `gl_bind_framebuffer:5`. A targeted default-framebuffer restore keeps the launcher readback on the presented surface after the FBO experiment. This is still not a Roblox-rendered sign-in screen: the remaining renderer blocker in this shaped app-pump lane is moving from smoke shader/state coverage to the complete live client material/shader pipeline, while the visible login route is still WebView bridge state rather than native Roblox-authored UI.
- `PURIFIED_APP_PUMP_STEADY_FRAMES=1` extends the live-app pump to 450 calls without changing the default launcher path. It repeats the real app-surface update, game-surface update, Roblox present-tail, first-frame schedule, and main-thread drain cycle until it reaches 33 `eglSwapBuffers` calls, 39 host native-window wrapper creations, fifteen first-frame schedules, 52 main-thread drains, 103 queued/drained host items, 66 host app-event dispatches, thirteen app-ready/game-loaded AppBridge event pairs, and fifteen Java-style first-frame callback deliveries with zero signal/abort recovery. The app-pump path now dispatches the initial AppBridge calls by symbol name, so Roblox sees typed fake Java models for `AppBridgeV2InitParams`, `StartAppParams`, and `StartGameParams` instead of generic/string fallthrough handles; structured telemetry reports `host_start_game_params=getters:22 place_id:1818 user_id:1 join_type:0 last:vrContext`. This gives the project a stable "keep Roblox alive and presenting" lane for the next window/UI work instead of only a short app-start burst.
- `PURIFIED_APP_PUMP_EXTRA_STEADY_CYCLES=N` is an opt-in stress knob for the same live app/game surface pulse. Each extra cycle appends app-ready, game-loaded, first-frame schedule, main-thread drains, app-surface update, game-surface update, and two Roblox present-tail swaps without changing the default launcher contract. A 10-cycle run currently reaches 550 native calls, 53 `eglSwapBuffers`, 25 first-frame schedules/callback deliveries, 82 main-thread drains, 153 queued/drained host records, 106 host app-event dispatches, and 23 app-ready/game-loaded AppBridge event pairs with zero signal/abort recovery. This is the fastest local way to test whether new Android/renderer bridge work survives a longer Roblox-owned surface loop.
- `PURIFIED_APP_PUMP_INTERACTION_CYCLES=1` appends an Android-style resize/keyboard/input pulse after each extra steady cycle. With `PURIFIED_REAL_INPUT_EVENTS=keyboard`, the bridge lets Roblox execute the stable resize/keyboard-size native handlers while keeping direct text/key/return/focus callbacks modeled as host input events. A four-cycle interaction run reaches 518 native calls, 41 `eglSwapBuffers`, 19 first-frame callback deliveries, 68 main-thread drains, 123 drained queue records, 82 app-event dispatches, and 17 app-ready/game-loaded pairs with zero signal/abort recovery. Full `PURIFIED_REAL_INPUT_EVENTS=1` is intentionally left as a crash-finding mode: direct `nativePassText`, `nativePassKeyEvent`, return, and focus calls currently fault because they need more real Android input/editor state than the bridge models.
- `PURIFIED_APP_PUMP_REAL_LIFECYCLE=1` appends a stable real fragment/session/surface lifecycle pulse after each extra steady cycle. The exported `nativeOnExperienceSessionResume`/`nativeOnExperienceSessionPause` entries in this APK begin with branches into an Android-seeded `.bss` island, so the probe repairs those public branch-island entries to safe returns before calling them. A two-cycle run now reaches 488 native calls, executes real exported `nativeOnFragmentStart` twice, crosses repaired exported `nativeOnExperienceSessionResume` twice without recovery, then follows each lifecycle pulse with main-thread drain, real `nativeAppBridgeV2ResumeGameWithPlatformParams`, app-surface update, game-surface update, and Roblox present-tail swaps. The current clean sample reports 49 host native-window wrappers, 41 `eglSwapBuffers`, 17 first-frame schedules, 62 main-thread drains, 119 queued/drained host records, and 15 app-ready/game-loaded pairs with zero signal/abort recovery. The deeper internal experience-session helper still needs a shaped native session object before it can do useful work beyond this export-level crossing.
- `PURIFIED_APP_PUMP_REAL_LIFECYCLE=deep` adds an opt-in internal experience-session lane. The probe now shapes a native session wrapper, reference-counted peer, app-state/data-model pair, and `eDserializePacketsJob` short-string message, then crosses Roblox's internal session resume helper at `0x2ba9978` and the packet-job gate at `0x2363c2c`. `PURIFIED_DEEP_EXPERIENCE_STATE=1 PURIFIED_DEEP_EXPERIENCE_PACKET_JOB=1` currently reaches 481 calls with `470:helper:ok`, `477:gate:ok`, one internal task worker drain/condition wait, 40 Roblox present-tail swaps, and a fully flushed host queue (`enqueued:114 drained:114 pending:0`) without signal/abort recovery. This is still not the sign-in UI: it proves the native experience job path can be entered and drained, but the shaped data-model is not complete enough for Roblox to enqueue new UI/material GL beyond the existing app-pump renderer coverage.
- The custom loader now resolves the Android NDK imports Roblox declares from the desktop host path itself, not only from the standalone shim/probe executable. This includes `ALooper_*`, `AAsset*`, `AConfiguration*`, `AMediaFormat*`, `AMediaCodec*`, and `android_set_abort_message`. The in-loader `ALooper` model is prepared before the app-pump sequence, supports multiple fd registrations, callback fds, callback auto-removal, and timeout/error return shapes; this removes a real discrepancy where `android-loader-probe` had shim symbols linked but the normal `purified-host` launcher did not.
- The fake JNI table now records `RegisterNatives` calls instead of blindly returning success. Reports include the registered class, method name, signature, function address, and library vaddr for the latest captured native method. Current `JNI_OnLoad` probing still does not reach a registration table before the known guarded JNI signal boundary, so hidden Java-registered surface methods such as `onSurfaceCreatedNative` are not callable through this mechanism yet. This makes the next boundary explicit: either advance JNI/class initialization far enough to see those registrations, or recover their native addresses through static analysis and call them as a controlled surface-lifecycle experiment.
- `scripts/dex-native-report.py` parses local DEX files without external tooling and lists native Java declarations with JNI-style names. The current APK declares the app/game surface path as `NativeGLInterface.nativeAppBridgeV2UpdateSurfaceAppWithPlatformParams`, `nativeAppBridgeV2UpdateSurfaceGameWithPlatformParams`, and `nativeAppBridgeV2ResumeGameWithPlatformParams`, plus fragment/session lifecycle exports such as `nativeOnFragmentStart` and `nativeOnExperienceSessionResume`. The hidden `onSurfaceCreatedNative`/`onSurfaceChangedNative` strings found in `libroblox.so` are not normal DEX-native methods in this APK build, so the fastest accurate route remains the exported `NativeGLInterface` surface/lifecycle bridge unless later static analysis recovers a callable internal address.
- The desktop loader now models Android logging and system properties (`__android_log_*`, `__system_property_get`) and prints `android_runtime=...` telemetry when Roblox calls them. The current app-pump launcher path does not hit these APIs, so the surface/UI stall is not yet exposing a normal Android log message in this lane.
- The visible host WebView login overlay is now opt-in with `PURIFIED_HOST_WEBVIEW_OVERLAY=1`. By default, the SDL window presents the Roblox GL-driven shared Android surface without covering it with the diagnostic website-style login panel. The WebView/login bridge state is still modeled and reported, but it no longer masks the surface we need to inspect for real Roblox-authored UI frames.
- `PURIFIED_REAL_GLES_BACKEND=1` enables an experimental Mesa/EGL pbuffer backend. It creates a real OpenGL ES context, forwards viewport/clear/depth/stencil/blend/cull/scissor state, translates Roblox guest GL object ids to real Mesa shader/program/buffer/texture/framebuffer/renderbuffer ids, forwards shader/program lifecycle, framebuffer/renderbuffer binding and attachment, buffer/texture allocation and parameter state, and reads the pbuffer back into the Android surface on each Roblox `eglSwapBuffers` present-tail call. The fake EGL layer now tracks current draw/read surfaces and current context, answers query-string/config/query-context/surface-attribute/current-surface calls for both direct imports and `eglGetProcAddress`, and clears current handles on unbind/destroy. The backing pbuffer follows Android surface geometry changes from `ANativeWindow_setBuffersGeometry` and present-time surface state, so a Roblox-driven resize can recreate/make-current a same-context Mesa surface before readback. On the current host the steady launcher path reaches `real_gles_backend=enabled:1 ready:1 presents:33 readbacks:33` with zero signal/abort recovery, and the strict render-pump path reaches shader/program/framebuffer/texture/buffer object mapping plus one Mesa-backed readback present.
- `PURIFIED_REAL_GLES_FORWARD_DRAWS=1` is a guarded opt-in on top of `PURIFIED_REAL_GLES_BACKEND=1`. It forwards vertex attribute state and `glDrawArrays`/`glDrawElements` when the current program and vertex buffer are mapped host objects, adopts unknown guest buffer ids into Mesa objects for the experimental path, retains the last valid host program across guest program unbinds, and falls back to the software surface marker for draws that still are not complete. This gives a safe experiment lane for real Roblox draw submission without making the default launcher depend on unsafe guest pointer forwarding.
- `PURIFIED_REAL_GLES_FORWARD_UPLOADS=1` is a second guarded opt-in. It tracks host allocations created by the probe allocator, trusted mapped/native-image spans, bridge-owned static spans, and bounded readable stack spans. Stack/readable upload data is staged into host-owned memory before forwarding to Mesa, so transient Roblox/probe upload payloads can cross into the real GLES backend without direct unsafe lifetime assumptions. It also forwards copy-texture operations and safe compressed texture allocation calls through the real GLES context.
- The launcher now prints `real_gles_activity=...` and `real_gles_state=...` next to `real_gles_backend=...`, so Orange Pi runs can confirm draw/upload/readback forwarding and see whether a frame used real Roblox GL state or bridge fallback state from the normal `./launch.sh` path. `REAL_GLES=1 ./launch.sh` is a shorthand for enabling `PURIFIED_REAL_GLES_BACKEND=1`, `PURIFIED_REAL_GLES_FORWARD_DRAWS=1`, and `PURIFIED_REAL_GLES_FORWARD_UPLOADS=1`; `REAL_GLES=0 ./launch.sh` forces the older placeholder-safe window path. The launcher default is now `REAL_GLES=auto`: real GLES is enabled for normal desktop video drivers and kept disabled for `VIDEO=dummy` unless explicitly forced. With `REAL_GLES=1 VIDEO=dummy ./launch.sh --frames 1 --screenshot /tmp/purified-real-gles-live.bmp`, the app-pump window path reports `real_gles_backend=enabled:1 ready:1`, `real_gles_activity=upload_forward:4 upload_skip:0 draw_forward:3 draw_skip:0 read_pixel_forward:0 read_pixel_skip:0`, `real_gles_state=program_adopt:0 buffer_adopt:1 texture_adopt:0 real_buffer_upload:1 real_texture_upload:3 synthetic_buffer_upload:0 synthetic_shader:0 synthetic_vertex:0`, `real_gles_best_readback=hash:41e5bec5 nonzero:64 unique:1`, and `host_surface_content=source:native_gles_readback`.
- The real GLES report now emits `real_gles_activity=...` and `real_gles_state=...` telemetry. Current strict render-pump telemetry with draw forwarding is `upload_forward:0 upload_skip:0 draw_forward:3 draw_skip:0 read_pixel_forward:1 read_pixel_skip:0 readback_nonzero:14 readback_unique:2`; with upload forwarding it is `upload_forward:4 upload_skip:0 draw_forward:3 draw_skip:0 read_pixel_forward:1 read_pixel_skip:0 readback_nonzero:14 readback_unique:2`, including one real buffer upload, three texture uploads, and one forwarded `glReadPixels(2x2 RGBA8)` into a guarded writable Roblox output buffer. The live app-pump lane now treats a Roblox GL buffer object with proven real storage as drawable storage when Roblox rebinds that same object across uniform/array/element targets, so it no longer has to inject synthetic shader, vertex, index, or buffer fallback for the visible app-pump draw path. The child process writes the best nonblank GLES readback to `/tmp/purified-real-gles-readback.bmp`, and the SDL host imports that BMP into the host Android surface before first presentation, so the normal Linux window/screenshot path can display the best bridged GLES readback instead of losing it to later present-tail clears. This is still not full Roblox-authored UI rendering; the remaining work is replacing shaped smoke calls with complete Roblox state from the live client pipeline.
- `REAL_ANDROID_LOOP=1 ./launch.sh` is a shorthand for the stronger experimental Android loop: it disables the older first-frame work-boundary substitute and enables `PURIFIED_REAL_FIRST_FRAME_SCHEDULER=1`, `PURIFIED_REAL_MAIN_THREAD_MESSAGES=1`, and `PURIFIED_REAL_MAIN_THREAD_MESSAGES_HOST_DRAIN=1`. With `REAL_ANDROID_LOOP=1 REAL_GLES=1 VIDEO=dummy ./launch.sh --frames 1`, the launcher path now runs 450 native app-pump calls with zero guarded recovery, 33 Roblox swaps, 36 Mesa readbacks, 18 real first-frame schedules, 18 `Runnable.run()` deliveries, and host-equivalent WebLogin/MessageBus queue delivery. The default launcher still keeps the older host main-thread drain until the internal Roblox queue/renderer job contract is modeled well enough to make the stronger path the normal default.
- The dynamic GLES loader contract is now explicit. With `PURIFIED_TRACE_EGL_GETPROC=1`, the app-pump probe shows every Roblox-requested `eglGetProcAddress` GL name resolving to a specific bridge handler instead of the generic `probe_gl_void` fallback. The covered set includes VAOs, instanced draws, draw buffers, clear-buffer calls, 3D/immutable texture calls, compressed 3D texture calls, framebuffer texture layers, framebuffer blits/invalidation, uniform-block queries/bindings, map/unmap buffer, sync objects, query objects, program binary hooks, debug labels/groups, buffer storage, copy-image, and multisample renderbuffer storage. This removes a class of silent no-op renderer capability failures before we move from shaped renderer smoke calls toward the live Roblox material pipeline.
- The launcher accepts `--screenshot PATH` and captures the first presented frame when used with `--frames 1`. This gives a repeatable Orange Pi/headless validation command: `./launch.sh --frames 1 --screenshot /tmp/purified-visible.bmp`.
- `NATIVE_SEQUENCE_MODE=render-pump` reaches a 529-entry render-pump native sequence with zero signal/abort recovery, seven host native-window wrapper creations, 12 host app-event dispatches, app pause/destroy lifecycle handlers, foreground lifecycle, legacy Lua-app startup, MainGameActivity startup hooks, APK asset-manager readiness, graphics-readiness/device-display/GameActivity handoff, cookie calls, logging calls, 42 app-shell protocol calls, media picker init/response, local secure-storage/user/delete calls, system-dialog host callback calls, NativeSettings bootstrap/config/dev/crash calls, NativeReporting foreground/background/session/push/stopwatch calls, purchase bridge callbacks, NativeGL input/back/text/runtime analytics and state hooks, all 37 exported `NativeInputInterface` gamepad/touch/mouse/sensor/screen-orientation hooks, flags, asset/local storage setup, audio/video/FMOD, dev branch, memstorage, meta attestation, app-hang monitor,
