Keep WebLoginProtocol emulation out of the native-login lane; it is a website bridge path, not the Roblox-created UI path the project is targeting.
- `native_login_queue_script`: added `scripts/probe_native_login_queue.sh` as the focused high-water target for future work. It pins the native-only Account didLogin + `user-init` + result-object app-target path and uses the richer result-target summary, including last UI record and word-15 callback histogram. Use this for post-reset producer experiments before falling back to the broader packet launcher.
- `startlua_boundary_capture`: `PURIFIED_PACKET_QUEUE_LATE_START_LUA_APP_HELPER=1 scripts/probe_native_login_queue.sh` confirms the app-target scout calls the internal StartLua boundary 225 times with a real DataModel-ish target (`arg0=<login-result+word4-adjacent>`, `arg2=1`) and paired slots pointing at the same dispatch target/ref pair. Replacing StartLua for capture suppresses UI enqueue to zero, which proves the 225 reset records are produced by Roblox's live StartLua body, not by the app-target wrapper itself. The captured slots are the next object-shape evidence for finding the post-reset LuaApp producer.
- `login_flags_report`: added `scripts/roblox-login-flags-report.sh` to extract login/auth defaults from the local APK dictionary. The cached defaults already enable the Lua/auth login path (`EnableAuthV2Login`, `EnablePlayerAppLoginPageExperiments`, `LuaAppLoginRollout=100`, `LuaAppLoginMethod=1`, `EnableLoginWithCredentials=100`, and related login revamp flags), so the reset-only blocker is probably not a missing static flag default. Runtime flag JNI modeling may still need exact key-aware returns if Roblox asks Java for live flag values.
- `late_login_arg_mode_sweep`: the late post-login scout now exposes `PURIFIED_PACKET_QUEUE_LATE_POST_LOGIN_ARG_MODE`, and `scripts/probe_login_arg_modes.sh` sweeps candidate function argument layouts. `user-init` remains the productive high-water candidate: default/app-target/dm-target shapes preserve 225 reset-only UI records, while app-dm-target and target-only regress. `lua-dm-login` stays at 97 reset-only records across the same shapes. This rules out a simple wrong argument order for the currently known direct login producers.
- `single_surface_login_observer`: `SingleSurfaceApp::userDidLogin` (`0x2581758`) reaches a virtual observer call at `target+960` vtable slot `+136`; the host shape now models that slot with `host_probe_login_virtual_notify` instead of a silent false stub and records the event. The real call reports event type `2` with payload `target+0x118`, proving Roblox emits a post-login observer notification on this path. Treating it as a state transition or pair drain still leaves the lane at 97 reset-only records, so the event is useful evidence but not itself the missing UI producer.
- `login_target_992_shape_negative_result`: `SingleSurfaceApp::userDidLogin` also calls an internal updater at `0x2581928` with `x0=[target+992]` and `x1=target+0x118`. The host shape now exposes `PURIFIED_LOGIN_TARGET_992_MODE=target|virtual|wrapper|zero`; target, virtual, and wrapper all remain at 97 reset-only UI records with the same observer event. The default high-water lane remains `user-init` -> outer result object -> app-target scout, not direct `userDidLogin`.
- `account_didlogin_handler_negative_result`: string-xrefs mapped `Account.didLogin` handlers at `0x22e020c` and `0x2c7c830`, `gameActivity_onDidLogInReceived` around `0x29fb70c`, and `PromptLogin` setup around `0x21260c4`. Direct probes of `0x2c7c830` and `0x29fb70c` with shaped login objects still produce only reset-state records and regress below the 225-record high-water mark. Keep these mapped as diagnostics; they are not yet better entry points than `UserController::initialize`.
- `native_login_highwater_fast_script`: added `scripts/probe_login_highwater.sh`, a narrow high-water wrapper for the current best Roblox-created packet lane. It pins Account `didLogin`, disables the WebView window path, uses `UserController::initialize` (`user-init`) with the productive app-target argument shape, and prints the UI enqueue owner snapshot. The control run preserves `constructors_attempted=762`, `ui_enqueue:225 records:225 ui_dispatch:225/512`, `packet_dispatch:731`, and the unique reset callback type. Use this script for day-to-day post-reset producer experiments before the broader launcher or packet queue scripts.
- `owner_and_route_bootstrap_negative_result`: UI enqueue telemetry now records the probable owner behind the repaired queue (`queue - 416`) plus shallow owner/deep-list fields. The high-water queue owner matches the `UserController::initialize` result-object path but currently has no deep producer list populated at that offset. Explicitly calling the static route/login setup blocks around `0x2006518` (`PlayerApp.Login`/Landing/Signup table setup) and `0x21260c4` (PromptLogin/LoginSucceeded registration area) before the productive `user-init` call preserves the 225 reset-only wave and does not produce PlayerApp UI callbacks. This rules out a missing one-shot route-table initializer as the immediate sign-in gate.
- `highwater_argument_and_pump_sweep`: `user-init` remains the only useful late producer. `app-target` and `dm-target` preserve the 225-record high-water wave, `app-dm-target` regresses to 193, and target-only/app-only/direct `lua-dm-login`/`lua-app-did-return` lanes regress to 97. Allowing the native-only WebView handshake and switching MessageBus auth payload to callback mode do not change the result. Aggressively lengthening the app pump hits the bounded native-sequence/alarm path before new accepted UI appears, so the next target is not "wait longer"; it is the missing post-reset DataModel/LuaApp producer or callback shape.
- `real_task_scheduler_boundary`: the high-water lane now has a real-dispatch scheduler variant. With `PURIFIED_PACKET_QUEUE_TASK_DISPATCH_HELPER=0`, shaped timers, and no seeded empty head record, Roblox's real dispatch helper runs stably: `q424/q432/q464/q1a8/q1b0/q1d0` are populated and the helper performs 1001 callback-copy calls, 998 after the external reset packet wave. This still remains reset-only, but it proves the prior all-zero task-dispatch view was partly a host queue-shape problem.
- `enqueue_to_drain_loss_boundary`: enqueue-tail telemetry shows Roblox enqueues one real task in the high-water lane: task words include a `0xfeedfacecafebeef` marker, a stack payload, and a callable live-image address at `0x2363bf0`. The later drain receives a different empty task record, so the missing sign-in producer is currently being lost between enqueue-tail and drain. Promoting the enqueue return into the synthetic queue head did not change the drain target; the next accurate work is preserving/copying the enqueued task record in the exact lock-free queue layout Roblox expects, or safely invoking the `0x2363bf0` callback with its captured payload.
- `scheduler_node_execution_boundary`: the enqueue-tail hook now records execute prerequisites and can directly invoke the real task runner at `0x2656bc0` with `PURIFIED_PACKET_QUEUE_TASK_QUEUE_EXECUTE_ENQUEUED_TASK=task|return`. Calling it on the original stack task enters the runner and guarded-faults through data-backed task contents; calling it on the returned 128-byte `TS::Step` node returns cleanly. That proves the returned node is the correct scheduler unit, but direct runner execution alone does not produce post-reset PlayerApp UI.
- `scheduler_erased_callback_boundary`: the returned node path now records the native callback globals at `0x67fb540` and `0x67fb548`. In the high-water lane both are null while the scheduler/free-list object at `0x67fb578` is live, so Roblox skips the erased-function invocation in both the post-enqueue caller and the task runner. An opt-in seed, `PURIFIED_PACKET_QUEUE_TASK_QUEUE_SEED_CALLBACK_GLOBALS=copy-run|run|copy`, can install nearby native helper candidates for guarded experiments; `copy-run` activates the path but faults inside data-backed task contents and remains reset-only. Keep this disabled by default. The next accurate step is to recover/build the correct erased-function manager/callable payload for the returned node, not simply force the callback globals.
- `login_only_surface_boundary`: added `PURIFIED_LOGIN_ONLY_SKIP_GAME_BRIDGE=1` and `scripts/probe_login_only_surface.sh` to isolate the logged-out app surface without `StartGame`, `UpdateSurfaceGame`, `ResumeGame`, `GameLoaded`, or experience lifecycle calls. The corrected skip path produces `game_loaded:0`, `route=PlayerApp.Login`, `surface=PlayerApp.Login`, and no native game-bridge fallthrough. The result is still exactly 97 external `GuiService::resetGuiStates(DataModel*)` records, while MessageBus asks for `WebViewProtocol/openWindow`. Expanding the forced login rollout/config lookup to match the APK dictionary (`LuaAppLoginMethod=1`, login rollouts `100`, auth/login revamp flags true) does not change the packet type. Current conclusion: this APK's logged-out credential entrypoint is WebLoginProtocol-driven; the native app surface is alive but still blocked before any post-reset PlayerApp UI producer emits non-reset callbacks.
- `playable_route_pivot`: the fastest path to playing a game is now explicitly WebView-auth-first, native-render-second. Added `./launch-play.sh` / `scripts/launch-play.sh`, which defaults to the WebKit login backend, WebViewProtocol handshake, modeled WebLogin/MessageBus callbacks, app/game bridge transitions, and real GLES. The WebKit helper now accepts `--cookie-output`, snapshots Roblox cookies without printing secret values, and writes a local `0600` cookie header file (default `/tmp/purified-roblox-cookie.txt`) that the play launcher reloads on subsequent runs. Once a cookie is available, the launcher automatically switches the modeled auth event from `web-login` to Account `didLogin`, and bridge summaries redact cookie values as `[redacted:...]`. `StartGameParams` string getters are now environment-backed (`PURIFIED_GAME_ID`, `PURIFIED_GAME_ACCESS_CODE`, `PURIFIED_GAME_LAUNCH_DATA`, `PURIFIED_GAME_JOIN_CONTEXT`, and related join fields), so the host can pass real session/place metadata instead of permanent empty strings. Smoke validation: `VIDEO=dummy REAL_GLES=0 ./launch-play.sh` reaches `host_session=webview:1 login_route:1 cookie:1 messagebus:1 app_shell:1 assets:1 webview_window:1`, `webview_host=ready:1 login_window:1 ... command:openWindow`, and typed `host_start_game_params=getters:22 place_id:1818 user_id:1`; an authenticated-cookie smoke reports Account `didLogin` and a redacted cookie summary.
- `persistent_cookie_store`: the WebView auth cookie header now defaults to `appData/roblox-cookie-header.txt` instead of `/tmp`, while the WebKit sqlite store remains `appData/webkit-cookies.sqlite`. `launch-play`, `launch-join`, and `launch-login` load the saved header automatically, migrate the old `/tmp/purified-roblox-cookie.txt` header if present, and only clear auth state when explicitly launched with `PURIFIED_RESET_COOKIES=1`. The WebKit login helper now seeds saved cookies as persistent cookies and, when a saved `.ROBLOSECURITY` exists, opens `https://www.roblox.com/home` instead of forcing the login route. Validation: `make -C build -j2 purified-host purified-webkit-login`, shell syntax checks for the launch scripts, and a dummy authenticated-cookie smoke confirms `cookie_file=loaded`, `mode:account`, and `cookie:1`.
- `accelerated_play_path`: the play/login path now prefers hardware compositing and avoids the slowest diagnostic readback behavior by default. `purified-webkit-login` sets WebKit hardware acceleration policy to `ALWAYS`, enables accelerated 2D canvas where the installed WebKitGTK exposes it, and prints `hardware_acceleration=always` on startup. `scripts/launch.sh` unsets accidental `LIBGL_ALWAYS_SOFTWARE=1` unless `PURIFIED_ALLOW_SOFTWARE_GL=1` is requested, and the normal play/default path now throttles Mesa GLES readback with `PURIFIED_REAL_GLES_READBACK_EVERY=30`. The GLES bridge still forwards Roblox draw/upload work to Mesa/Panfrost, but it no longer stalls on every present just to feed diagnostics. `./launch-gl.sh` and `./launch-native-packet.sh` keep `PURIFIED_REAL_GLES_READBACK_EVERY=1` for full pixel/readback verification. Validation: build passes, shell syntax checks pass, and a dummy real-GLES play smoke dropped from `presents:110 readbacks:110`-style behavior to `presents:110 readbacks:4` while preserving `host_surface_content=source:roblox_packet_readback`.
- `join_route_deeplink_boundary`: added `./launch-join.sh` / `scripts/launch-join.sh` as the focused playable-route lane. It reuses the cookie/WebView-auth infrastructure, then suppresses the login WebView, sets `PlayerApp.GameJoin.UX`, marks the StartApp model as a game launch, supplies a `robloxmobile://experiences/start?placeId=...` protocol URL, emits a shaped `WebLoginProtocolLaunchExperience` MessageBus payload, and can run the real `JNIWebLoginProtocol_maybeHandleColdStartProtocolLaunch` body with that deeplink (`PURIFIED_REAL_WEBLOGIN_COLD_START=1`). The bounded authenticated smoke reaches `host_session=webview:0 login_route:0 cookie:1 messagebus:1`, `host_app_bridge_events=... route:PlayerApp.GameJoin.UX context:GameJoin`, and typed `StartGameParams`, while staying stable under guarded signal recovery. The UI queue remains uniquely `GuiService::resetGuiStates(DataModel*)`, so route strings, WebView suppression, protocol deeplink shape, WebLoginProtocol launch-experience payload, and the real cold-start export are now ruled out as sufficient by themselves. The next fastest accurate target is the native place-launcher/join-script path (`GameJoinUtil::makePlaceLauncherRequest`, `/v1/join-game`, `joinScript`, `PlaceLauncherRequestResult`) rather than more login UI work.
- `experience_protocol_and_placeid_boundary`: `launch-join` now defaults to an `ExperienceProtocol/LaunchExperience` MessageBus shape instead of the older WebLogin launch-experience shape, while `web-launch-experience` remains available as the legacy fallback. Fast probes confirm the raw MessageBus protocol/method now report `protocol:ExperienceProtocol message:LaunchExperience`, callbacks can be invoked, and StartGameParams still report place `1818`, but native DNS/socket/connect counters remain zero. Added `./launch-place-notify.sh` / `scripts/launch-place-notify.sh` as the focused PlaceID handoff lane. It appends a bounded CallProtocol cycle that reaches `getPlaceIdKey`, `getCallIdKey`, `getGameInstanceIdKey`, `getReservedServerAccessCodeKey`, and `nativeBroadcastEventWithNamespace` cleanly in host mode; the remaining real `JNICallProtocol_receiveCall` handoff is the current specific signal blocker. A guessed real `nativeBroadcastEventWithNamespace("Game", "Start PlaceID", payload)` also signal-skips, so the next accurate work is recovering the exact Java-side CallProtocol/notification object shape or the native ExperienceProtocolCore launch handler ABI, not adding more generic GL/network stubs.
- `join_handoff_shape_sweep`: added a join-mode Lua config profile for game-join flags (`EnableIrisGameJoinAndroid2`, `EnableGameJoinContext`, `WhitelistGameJoinEndpoint`, related keys), changed `launch-join` to emit parser-friendly lowercase `placeid`/`id` protocol URLs, and added an opt-in `PURIFIED_MESSAGEBUS_AUTH_EVENT=account-then-experience` mode that feeds Account `didLogin` before switching to `ExperienceProtocol/LaunchExperience`. Controls with default ExperienceProtocol, legacy WebLogin launch, combined Account->Experience, skipped `gameLoaded`, immediate StartGame, real network-state delivery, and the focused PlaceID lane all still stop at the same boundary: typed `StartGameParams` are read, callbacks are invoked, but `getaddrinfo/socket/connect` remain zero and no `FetchedJoinScript`/`PlaceLauncher` telemetry appears. A direct `GameJoinUtil` scout at `0x2c0cdec` proved those string-xref targets are C++ instance functions requiring real Roblox owner objects; brute-calling them with host strings faults before useful work. The fastest next target is still the exact native owner/callback object that receives the Experience/CallProtocol launch and calls `GameJoinUtil::makePlaceLauncherRequest`.
- `experience_raw_launch_id_boundary`: added a guarded join-endpoint tripwire on the observed `/v1/join-game` native string-xref candidates and fixed `JNIExperienceProtocol_getLaunchId` to return the configured launch id. DEX mapping shows `Lsl/a` wires `JNIExperienceProtocol.getLaunchId()` into raw `MessageBus.u(launchId, callback)`, `Lsl/a$a` forwards `a(JSONObject)`, and the relevant Android callback path is `ActivityNativeMain.R0 -> ActivityNativeMain.j1 -> xg/l.b(JSONObject, Activity)`, which reads root-level `placeId`, `gameInstanceId`, `reservedServerAccessCode`, `launchData`, `gameJoinContext`, and `joinAttemptOrigin`. The bridge now uses the launch id as the raw topic and sends root-level ExperienceProtocol launch JSON for raw publish/subscribe instead of wrapping it as `{protocol, method, parameters}`. Focused `launch-join` probes with the tripwire still do not reach `/v1/join-game`, DNS, socket, or connect, so the remaining boundary is likely the real Java callback owner/activity handoff into `xg/l.j(...)` / `bi/k0`, not the raw topic or launch-object shape.
- `startgame_dispatch_boundary`: DEX mapping confirmed Android's deep-link route ends at the same JNI entry we already call: `ExperienceSession.l0() -> bi/i0.B(...) -> NativeGLInterface.nativeAppBridgeV2StartGameWithParam(StartGameParams)`. Native disassembly shows `StartGameWithParam` reads all 22 getters, builds the native start-game payload, then calls the shared app-event/task dispatcher at `0x29c8654`. Added opt-in tripwires for the native `/v1/join-game` sites, `joinScript` sites, StartGame call sites, and the shared dispatcher target. `PURIFIED_PATCH_SHARED_DISPATCH_TARGET_TRIPWIRE=1 ./launch-join.sh` proves Roblox reaches `AppBridgeV2/startGameDispatch` after typed `StartGameParams`; the normal join lane still does not hit join-script, endpoint, DNS, socket, or connect. A first forwarder experiment (`PURIFIED_FORWARD_APP_EVENT_DISPATCH=1`) replays the dispatcher body through its real internal queue functions but is not stable yet; it crashes before useful join telemetry when installed around the current app-event ordering. Next work is a safer trampoline/restore strategy for `0x29c8654` or direct preservation of the queued start-game task, not more Java payload shaping.
- `desktop_icon_support`: `purified.png` in the project root is now used as the SDL host window icon and the optional GTK WebView login window icon. `PURIFIED_ICON_PATH=/path/to/icon.png` can override it. The SDL path loads PNGs through Cairo and applies the icon immediately after `SDL_CreateWindow`; the GTK path applies the same icon on the WebKit login window. Because Wayland app bars usually ignore runtime window icons and instead match app id/class to an installed `.desktop` entry, the launcher now sets `SDL_APP_NAME`, `SDL_VIDEO_WAYLAND_WMCLASS=purified`, and `SDL_VIDEO_X11_WMCLASS=purified`, while WebKit sets the GTK program/application name and WM class. Added `packaging/purified.desktop` and `scripts/install-desktop-icon.sh`; normal non-dummy `./launch.sh` runs auto-install the user-local launcher/icon by default (`PURIFIED_AUTO_INSTALL_DESKTOP_ICON=0` disables it). `make -C build -j2 purified-host purified-webkit-login`, installer testing with temporary `XDG_DATA_HOME`, and a short `VIDEO=dummy ./launch.sh --frames 1` smoke all pass.
- `startgame_native_task_queue_boundary`: added focused app-event queue capture and worker-queue telemetry. `./launch-dispatch.sh` captures exactly one StartGame queued app event with `GameJoin.UX` string fragments, proving `nativeAppBridgeV2StartGameWithParam` reaches Roblox's native queued app-event boundary. With `PURIFIED_RAW_SURFACE_CONTEXT=1 ./launch-join.sh`, Roblox's real queue body now leaves `host_task_global=pending:0x7`, proving StartGame enqueues native worker tasks rather than stopping at Java/MessageBus shape. The decoded pending callback vaddrs are currently `0x29d6630`, `0x29d5378`, `0x2`, `0x25826a8`, `0x29d4f54`, `0x2251ac0`, and `0x22ffb78`.
- `single_task_exec_boundary`: added `./launch-task-exec.sh` to execute the newest queued worker task through Roblox's per-task executor at `0x22518ac` instead of forcing the full worker loop. This reaches the same specific crash as direct worker drain: `pc_vaddr=0x621e490`, `lr_vaddr=0x224943c`, with `x0/x21` containing ASCII-ish `000-400` data. Static inspection shows the newest callback `0x22ffb78` belongs to `nativeAppBridgeV2InitWithParams` follow-up/app-bridge singleton work and expects fully initialized bridge/string state. The next accurate target is not another Java payload; it is fixing the object/string state expected by that native task callback, or selecting/executing the correct earlier StartGame task from the seven-task queue in order.
- `native_gamejoin_launch_packet_boundary`: direct task execution now supports indexed/ranged runs through `PURIFIED_DIRECT_TASK_EXEC_INDEX` and `PURIFIED_DIRECT_TASK_EXEC_COUNT`, and `./launch-gamejoin-native.sh` pins the productive task window `4..6` with MessageBus callback invocation enabled. Added checked native shims for malformed Android/libc++ string state at `0x621e478`, `0x2249798`, and `0x2963ecc`, plus a narrow `PURIFIED_SKIP_APPBRIDGE_LAUNCH_CLEANUP=1` branch over the post-LaunchExperience temporary-object cleanup in task `0x22ffb78`. The focused lane now returns `native_sequence=ready`, `child_signal=0`, executes three Roblox queued native tasks, enters `PlayerApp.GameJoin.UX`, and emits Roblox's own `ExperienceProtocol/LaunchExperience` payload from the AppBridge path while invoking four shaped MessageBus callbacks. Controls with full seven-task execution, callback invocation, and real network-state delivery still report zero DNS/socket/connect activity, so the next accurate target is the Android callback owner/activity handoff that turns the LaunchExperience callback into the real place-launcher/join-script request.
- `post_experience_startgame_ordering`: DEX inspection confirmed the Android route is `sl/a$a.a(JSONObject) -> ActivityNativeMain.j1 -> xg/l.b -> xg/l.j -> xg/l.c -> game/c.q -> bi/i0.B -> nativeAppBridgeV2StartGameWithParam`. The launcher can now model that ordering with `PURIFIED_START_GAME_AFTER_EXPERIENCE_CALLBACK=1`, separated from the earlier app-event StartGame by `PURIFIED_DIRECT_TASK_EXEC_AFTER_INITIAL_START_GAME=0` and `PURIFIED_DIRECT_TASK_EXEC_AFTER_EXPERIENCE_CALLBACK=1`. This doubles StartGame getter coverage to 44, raises the native task queue from seven to fifteen pending tasks, and exposes new post-callback task continuations `0x22dcfa4`, `0x29d6dc8`, and `0x29d6eb4`. Executing the whole visible post-callback row and guarded real worker-drain remains stable but still does not reach DNS/socket/connect; the next boundary is preserving the real queue's lock-free dequeue semantics or the Java `game/c.q` owner state, not the raw ExperienceProtocol packet shape.
- `gamejoin_queue_tail_boundary`: task-queue telemetry now reports both the front and back of Roblox's global worker queue. This matters because the real drain pops newest/back, while the older report only sampled the oldest/front records. The post-Experience queue tail currently decodes as `0x22ffb78`, `0x2251ac0`, `0x29d4f54`, `0x25826a8`, `0x29d5b74`, an invalid `0x2` callback slot, `0x29d6630`, and `0x29d6eb4`. `./launch-gamejoin-native.sh` now defaults to post-callback StartGame ordering, skips early task execution, executes the newest visible tasks, and validates task callback vaddrs so the bad sentinel slot does not crash the lane. A guarded wide-tail control stays `native_sequence=ready` and reaches `direct:8`, but still reports zero DNS/socket/connect.
- `real_worker_drain_negative_result`: added opt-in post-Experience real worker restore/drain controls (`PURIFIED_DIRECT_TASK_WORKER_DRAIN_AFTER_EXPERIENCE_CALLBACK`, `PURIFIED_REAL_TASK_WORKER_DRAIN_AFTER_EXPERIENCE_CALLBACK`, and `PURIFIED_DIRECT_TASK_WORKER_DRAIN_AFTER_EXPERIENCE_CALLBACK_COUNT`). Even one real post-callback worker pop currently fails early in the native free/string path (`pc_vaddr=0x1f24c3c`, `lr_vaddr=0x1f24bcc`) before reaching the later AppBridge cycles. Keep real worker draining off by default. The accurate next step is to model or preserve the task node ownership/string lifetime expected by the worker queue, not to force the worker loop harder.
- `authenticated_gamejoin_user_boundary`: `launch-play` now derives `PURIFIED_GAME_USER_ID` / `PURIFIED_APP_USER_ID` from the persisted WebKit cookie header (`rbxuid`, then `rbxid`) instead of defaulting every native game launch to user id `1`. The native fallback does the same if a launcher forgets to export the ids, and cookie JNI getters now return the persisted cookie header rather than only recording telemetry. Stable `./launch-gamejoin-native.sh` now reports `host_start_game_params ... user_id:1302976792` for the current cookie while staying `native_sequence=ready`; DNS/socket/connect remain zero, so the authenticated id was necessary cleanup but not the missing join-script trigger. Added `scripts/dex-code-report.py`, a small local DEX method reporter, and used it to confirm the Android route: `Lxg/l.b(JSONObject, Activity)` parses root-level `placeId`, `gameInstanceId`, `reservedServerAccessCode`, `launchData`, `gameJoinContext`, and `joinAttemptOrigin`, then flows through `xg/l.j -> xg/l.c -> game/c.q -> ExperienceSession.l0 -> bi/i0.B -> nativeAppBridgeV2StartGameWithParam`. An opt-in real native cookie setter (`PURIFIED_REAL_COOKIE_SET_MULTIPLE=1`) can call Roblox's `nativeSetMultipleCookies`, but it is unstable today: it reaches full StartGame getter coverage and cookie telemetry, then exits through signal/illegal-instruction recovery and can corrupt the reported user id. Keep it off by default until the native cookie/string owner shape is modeled.
- `ordered_post_experience_task_lane`: task execution now reports direct-task callback vaddrs, results, and filter skips. Callback sweeps showed `0x25826a8`, `0x22dcfa4`, `0x234cde0`, `0x29d5378`, `0x29d5b74`, `0x29d4f54`, and `0x29d6eb4` are stable side-effect/materialization tasks, while broad sequences that include `0x2251ac0` or the cleanup callback `0x22ffb78` fall back into the real worker/string free crash at `pc_vaddr=0x1f24c3c`. `./launch-gamejoin-native.sh` now disables the duplicate early `StartGame`, defaults to the stable post-Experience allow-list `0x29d6630,0x29d4f54,0x22dcfa4,0x234cde0,0x29d6eb4`, and reaches seven guarded direct Roblox native tasks with full 44 StartGame getter coverage. DNS/socket/connect are still zero, so the next accurate target is stabilizing the `nativeGameGlobalInit` follow-up/owner state (`0x2251ac0`) or modeling the Java `ExperienceSession.l0` setup around `NativeGLInterface.nativeGameGlobalInit()` before native StartGame.
- `game_global_init_followup_boundary`: static DEX inspection of `ExperienceSession.l0()` shows Android calls `NativeGLInterface.nativeGameGlobalInit()` immediately before constructing `bi/i0` and calling `nativeAppBridgeV2StartGameWithParam`. The `0x2251ac0` queued callback is in the `nativeGameGlobalInit` follow-up region and faults if the real worker/string owner path is forced, but marking its once flag (`PURIFIED_GAME_GLOBAL_INIT_FOLLOWUP_DONE=1`) lets it return cleanly. `./launch-gamejoin-native.sh` now defaults to that model and includes `0x2251ac0`, reaching eight guarded native tasks while staying `native_sequence=ready`. It still reports zero DNS/socket/connect, so `0x2251ac0` is not the join trigger by itself; the remaining blocker is the Java `ExperienceSession`/activity owner state or the real join-script fetch path feeding native with a completed place-launch result.
- `corrected_ghidra_gamejoin_boundary`: local Ghidra work must account for the imported image base (`0x100000`). Runtime vaddrs need `+0x100000` for Ghidra addresses; earlier raw string/candidate decompiles without that adjustment were shifted. Added `tools/ghidra_scripts/JoinBoundaryReport.java`, `RefsForAddresses.java`, and `FindFunctionsByName.java` for focused headless reports. Corrected decompile/objdump inspection proves `0x2c0cdec` is a real `/v1/join-game` place-launcher helper, but its ABI is `GameJoinState/shared-owner pair` in `x0` plus a DataModel/config pointer in `x1`, not raw URL/payload strings. Its caller at `0x2c0cb1c` builds the needed state then calls `0x2c0cdec`; direct fake-string scouts returning `0x2` are therefore expected and should not be treated as useful join execution.
- `gamejoin_all_task_negative_result`: added `PURIFIED_DIRECT_TASK_EXEC_ALLOW_ALL=1` and `./launch-gamejoin-alltasks.sh` for aggressive guarded post-Experience task execution. The lane executes all visible queued tasks (`direct:16`, or `direct:24` with eight guarded worker-drain passes) with no filter skips and still does not hit `Purified_internal_join_endpoint_tripwire`, `joinScript`, DNS, socket, or connect. A richer StartGameParams shape (`joinRequestType=1`, event/call/join attempt ids, referral, and join context) changes getter telemetry but does not enter GameJoinUtil. Conclusion: the next fix is a purpose-built `state+1024` owner/dispatcher model for the `0x2c8a41c` transition packet, not the real dispatcher object, not packet-helper replay, and not more route-string shaping.
- `appbridge_slot56_forwarding_and_slot80_route_update`: forwarding the exact post-copy slot `+56` caller at `0x2c8a41c` through the generic app-event dispatcher is now closed out as a negative in both modes. Immediate forwarding and forced queued forwarding both stay alive but move the guarded fault into the nullable clone/copy helper (`pc_vaddr=0x2a16c48`, `lr_vaddr=0x2a15a14`) and still report `socket:0 connect:0`. The better result is the now-reachable slot `+80` continuation: added `./launch-gamejoin-appqueue-slot80-route.sh`, which combines the original singleton `+184` body with `PURIFIED_APPBRIDGE_SLOT80_CALL_DIRECT_PACKET_LR=0x2c8e75c`. That lane stays `native_sequence=ready`, preserves GameJoin/GLES/readback, reaches `host_experience_route ... start_game:1`, and reads authenticated `StartGameParams` (`place_id:1818`, user id from cookie/env). It still does not reach join parent/endpoint or sockets. Enabling the StartGame dispatch tripwire proves the next boundary at `host_start_game_dispatch` (`lr_vaddr=0x29cecec`, `arg1=0`), but calling the original `0x29c8654` continuation under `PURIFIED_START_GAME_DISPATCH_CALL_ORIGINAL=1` currently hangs before the normal harness report. Next target: model the lower consumer of the StartGame dispatch packet/owner after `0x29c8654`, not another slot-56 forwarder.
- `startgame_dispatch_forward_milestone`: added `PURIFIED_START_GAME_DISPATCH_FORWARD_EVENT`, `PURIFIED_START_GAME_DISPATCH_FORWARD_LIMIT`, and `./launch-gamejoin-startdispatch-forward.sh`. This is the first stable lane that forwards the real native StartGame dispatch packet at `0x29cecec` into Roblox's queued app-event dispatcher and gets real `appEventDispatchForward:ok` returns while preserving the broad GameJoin/GLES/readback lane. The unlimited experiment proved the dispatcher consumes the packet repeatedly (`host_app_event_queue:capture:2364`) but eventually crashes in a later Android-dependent worker path; the limited launcher now defaults to 32 forwards and returns `native_sequence=ready`, `child_signal=0`, `gl:257`, `egl_swap:107`, `host_app_event_queue:capture:32`, packet readback, and no sockets/connect yet. Calling the dispatcher's real `pthread_once` initializer is unsafe in the current partial runtime and can fail early, so the stable path uses the already-initialized dispatcher slot only. This moves the playability blocker past "can we feed the StartGame dispatch packet?" and onto "what downstream owner/task state must the consumed packet produce before `/v1/join-game` networking starts?"
- `slot80_startdispatch_forward_highwater`: added `./launch-gamejoin-slot80-startdispatch-forward.sh` as the current focused GameJoin high-water target. It combines the original singleton slot `+184` body, the slot `+80` route handoff, bounded forwarding of the real StartGame dispatch packet, late app-event queue sampling, and behavior-preserving wrappers for singleton slots `+152` and `+160`. Verified result remains stable with `native_sequence=ready`, `child_signal=0`, `gl:257`, `egl_swap:107`, Roblox packet readback, `host_app_event_queue:capture:94`, `host_start_game_dispatch:calls:34`, and no socket/connect. The late queue samples now decode the downstream GameJoin packets as `PlayerApp.GameJoin.UX` payloads, and the exact singleton consumers are confirmed: slot `+160` enters original body `0x2c8b4c4` from the `0x29d53f0` path, while slot `+152` enters original body `0x2c8b5fc` from the `0x29d5bf4` path. Static inspection of those bodies shows both submit stack packets to the modeled AppBridge `state+960` dispatcher at vtable slot `+136` (`0x2c8b534` / `0x2c8b674`) and then clean up retained string state. The next target is therefore the semantic implementation of dispatcher slot `+136` for these GameJoin packets, not more route strings, StartGameParams, or blind callback widening.
- `slot136_filtered_forward_boundary`: added an LR-filtered, count-limited mode for `PURIFIED_APPBRIDGE_FORWARD_DISPATCH_SLOT136` plus `./launch-gamejoin-slot136-forward.sh`. Forwarding only the newly identified GameJoin slot `+136` stack-packet sites (`0x2c8b674` or `0x2c8b534`) with limit `4` is stable enough to preserve the app pump, GLES, and packet readback while raising app-event queue captures from `94` to `98`. Both sibling sites converge on the same next blocker: a nullable clone/copy helper fault around `pc_vaddr=0x2a16c40..0x2a16c50` / `lr_vaddr=0x2a15a14`, with no socket/connect and only zero-arg join-state/upstream counters. Static inspection shows `0x2a15a14` calls `0x2251294` after `0x2a16c20` clones the optional object at `[packet + 32]`: if it is neither null nor self, Roblox calls that object's vtable slot `+16`; if it is self, it uses vtable slot `+24` with the destination retbuf. This proves the immediate missing model is the optional clone/copy object shape consumed after the slot `+136` GameJoin packet forward, not the route, web auth, or the singleton slot `+152/+160` call itself.
- `slot136_optional_clear_milestone`: `./launch-gamejoin-slot136-forward.sh` now defaults `PURIFIED_APPBRIDGE_FORWARD_DISPATCH_SLOT136_CLEAR_OPTIONAL=1`, forwarding a shadow copy of the slot `+136` stack packet with word 4 (`[packet + 32]`) cleared while leaving Roblox's original stack packet untouched. This removes the `0x2a16c40..0x2a16c50` clone/copy fault and keeps GameJoin/GLES/readback alive. Forwarding both sibling packet sites together (`PURIFIED_APPBRIDGE_FORWARD_DISPATCH_SLOT136_LR=0`, limit `8` or `16`) raises app-event queue captures to `102`; the run then returns to the known worker-task executor boundary at `0x225192c`, where the task pointer decodes to `FatalRun...timeError...` fragments instead of a valid task object. Added opt-in `PURIFIED_PATCH_TASK_EXEC_SAFE_SHIM=1` and `PURIFIED_TASK_EXEC_SAFE_SHIM_SKIP_CALLBACKS=1` diagnostics. Calling callback-backed tasks through the shim advances captures to `104` but jumps through a non-image host-shaped callback; skipping all task callbacks regresses the AppBridge path, proving those callbacks are required. Next target: model the callback-backed worker task objects produced after the accepted slot `+136` packets, not skipping the worker queue globally.
- `worker_callback_shape_capture`: task-queue telemetry now records the first eight words of each sampled callback object. In the clean slot `+136` optional-clear lane, the front queue is dominated by `0x29d6eb4` callbacks whose objects share the shape `vtable, appbridge_state, 0x3f800000, flags, marker/optional, payload_or_state, 0, 0`; the back queue also exposes `PlayerApp.GameJoin.UX` string-backed callback objects plus repeated surface/app packet callback shapes. The vtable pointers resolve into relocated `.data.rel.ro` around `0x633f068`, `0x633f168`, and `0x633f468`, so the next implementation should model or preserve these callback object vtables/fields. This is more precise than trying to skip the whole task executor: the worker callbacks are required, but the current host-shaped callback ownership is still incomplete.
- `webkit_deeplink_intercept`: the WebKit play/login helper now intercepts Roblox app deep links instead of letting WebKit display a "URL cannot be shown" error. Navigation to `roblox://...`, `robloxmobile://...`, direct `https://www.roblox.com/games/start?...`, mobile `https://ro.blox.com/...` AppsFlyer redirects, and the Play Store fallback `market://details?id=com.roblox.client&referrer=...` is logged as `deeplink_intercepted`, ignored by the browser, and written to `appData/webkit-deeplink.txt` with both `original=` and `normalized=` lines. The real Play button flow now normalizes to `robloxmobile://experiences/start?placeId=...&joinAttemptId=...&joinAttemptOrigin=PurifiedWebView`, preserving `joinAttemptId` through nested/double-encoded referrers. `./launch-join.sh` loads the saved normalized deep link when `PURIFIED_PROTOCOL_LAUNCH_URL` is not explicitly set. Validation: targeted rebuild of `purified-webkit-login`; CLI normalization checks for direct `/games/start`, `ro.blox.com`, `market://details`, and `roblox://experiences/start`; shell syntax checks for `launch-play`, `launch-login`, and `launch-join`. A tool-launched real `./launch-play.sh` could not create GTK (`gtk_init failed`) because the tool shell lacks the desktop display environment, so the final browser-click validation must be run from the user's terminal.
- `real_web_play_deeplink_to_startgame`: validated the WebKit Play button path against the user's real log. `./launch-play.sh` now captures repeated `https://ro.blox.com/Ebh5?...` launch redirects and writes the latest normalized mobile URL. `./launch-join.sh` now parses that saved URL into `PURIFIED_GAME_PLACE_ID`, `PURIFIED_GAME_JOIN_ATTEMPT_ID`, `PURIFIED_GAME_JOIN_ATTEMPT_ORIGIN`, `PURIFIED_GAME_ID`, `PURIFIED_EXPERIENCE_LAUNCH_ID`, and `PURIFIED_GAME_CALL_ID` before building StartGame params. The native report now reaches `host_start_game_params place_id:10449761463 user_id:1302976792`, `gameId:10449761463`, real `joinAttemptId`, real `callId`, and an ExperienceProtocol `LaunchExperience` payload carrying the same launch id/join attempt id. Validation: `sh -n scripts/launch-join.sh`, targeted rebuild of `purified-host`, default join lane, and the current slot-136 high-water lane. This is a launch-correctness milestone, not a playability milestone: `socket:0 connect:0` remains, and the high-water lane still returns to the `0x225192c` worker-task object boundary after accepted slot-136 GameJoin packets. A focused `0x29d6eb4` task-safe-shim experiment was stable but regressive (`host_app_event_queue` dropped from 98 to 56), so the next target remains modeling/preserving the callback-backed worker task objects rather than directly executing only that callback.
- `slot136_worker_callback_vtable_map`: task telemetry now records callback vtables and the first eight vtable slots for front/back global task samples. In the real deeplink slot-136 high-water lane, the front queue resolves to the `0x29d6eb4` vtable slots (`0x29c836c,0x29d6e38,0x29d6e3c,0x29d6e84,0x29d6eac,0x29d6eb0,0x29d6eb4,0x29d6ef4`), while the back queue exposes `PlayerApp.GameJoin.UX` callbacks (`0x29d5378` family) and the surface/app packet callback family (`0x25826a8`). Static disassembly confirms the generic task executor at `0x22518ac` invokes `[task+32]` via vtable slot `+48`, then destroys `[task+48]`. A stricter heap-task plausibility filter now keeps future direct task drains from accepting image/text fragments as task objects. Negative controls: draining from the back of the queue regressed app-event captures from 98 to 33, deferring StartGame until app events reached only 64 captures, and restoring Roblox's real task worker during StartGame reached only 47 captures; all still reported `socket:0 connect:0`. The current best route remains the slot-136 optional-clear forwarder with front-queue semantics. Next target: model the object at `state+1024` / AppBridge worker owner so those vtable-backed callbacks are consumed by the right Roblox owner instead of the generic worker executor boundary.
- `state1024_split_and_state992_appstart_boundary`: AppBridge state bootstrap now supports a split synthetic `state+1024` owner (`PURIFIED_APPBRIDGE_1024_OWNER_MODE=split`) with owner-tagged dispatch telemetry. This proved slot `+136` GameJoin submits are still owned by the generic `state+960` dispatcher (`owner:0x1`), while `state+1024` is hit through slot `0` callbacks (`owner:0x2`). Widening the guarded safe front-task window from 64 to 160 raises app-event captures from `100` to `194` and reaches the native AppBridge app-start handoff at `0x23efc80`, where Roblox dereferenced null `state+992`. The opt-in `PURIFIED_APPBRIDGE_SEED_STATE_992=1` installs a zeroed owner object large enough for the immediate `+288` read, removing that null fault and moving the blocker to a deliberate app-start exception trap (`pc_vaddr=0x2ba04e4`, `lr_vaddr=0x234cedc`). Added `./launch-gamejoin-state992-160.sh` as the focused high-water diagnostic for this lane. The trap-return bypass is negative: it falls into a heap/non-image pointer and still reports `socket:0 connect:0`, so keep it disabled. Next target: recover the real app-start owner/params contract behind `state+992` and the `0x234cde0..0x234cf34` `nativeAppBridgeStartLuaAppDM` path, not a broad exception bypass.
- `appstart_dns_frontier_and_apk_update_lane`: added `scripts/update-roblox-apk-side-by-side.sh` for safe current-APK comparison without replacing the active `roblox_2.718.1110-an1.com` tree. The focused state+992 lane now carries the productive app-start fixes: skip the first nullable app-start cleanup virtual call (`PURIFIED_PATCH_APPSTART_CLEANUP_VCALL_NOP=1`), guard malformed libc++ string copies (`PURIFIED_PATCH_LIBCPP_STRING_COPY_SAFE=1`), and skip the two Android-only null queue/callback provider reads (`PURIFIED_PATCH_APPSTART_NULL_QUEUE_SKIP=1`, `PURIFIED_PATCH_APPSTART_NULL_CALLBACK_SKIP=1`). This removes the prior app-start trap/string/null-provider blockers and reaches the first native networking boundary in the GameJoin path: `native_network=getaddrinfo:2`, with real Play-button metadata still intact (`place_id:10449761463`, authenticated user id, joinAttemptId, `PlayerApp.GameJoin.UX`, real GLES/readback). It does not yet reach `socket` or `connect`; the DNS calls currently show null node/service and may be a readiness probe rather than `/v1/join-game`. Added `./launch-gamejoin-network-frontier.sh` as a faster wrapper for this lane, though the current stop-after-DNS hook does not terminate this app-pump path yet. Negative controls: replacing the shared hash lookup helper or skipping the `app_duration_adjust` lookup site avoids the later `0x221d5d4` telemetry/config-map fault but regresses the worker task queue to `direct:0`, so both remain opt-in diagnostics (`PURIFIED_PATCH_HASH_STRING_LOOKUP_SAFE=0`, `PURIFIED_PATCH_APP_DURATION_LOOKUP_NULL=0`). Current frontier: preserve the productive `direct:160` queue and model the app-start/telemetry map owner without emptying downstream GameJoin tasks.
- `slot184_forward_callback_clone_boundary`: network reporting now records `getaddrinfo` return code and result count. The current state+992 lane reports `getaddrinfo:2 rc:0 results:0 socket:0 connect:0`, confirming the first DNS boundary is still a readiness/probe stage rather than the actual game-server connection. Static inspection mapped the two productive direct callbacks: `0x25826a8` branches through AppBridge singleton slot `+176`, and `0x29d6eb4` branches through singleton slot `+184`. Added a corrected-but-disabled hash lookup model and an exact empty-bucket signal-resume experiment; both remain negative because they collapse the productive direct queue. Added `./launch-gamejoin-slot184-forward.sh`, which captures and forwards slot `+184` while still allowing the original body. This raises app-event capture to about `253` and preserves GameJoin metadata, real GLES, and packet readback, but moves the frontier to the callback clone/copy helper (`pc_vaddr=0x2a16c48`, `lr_vaddr=0x2a15a14`) before any socket/connect. Next target: model the optional callback object copied by `0x2a16c20` / consumed after slot `+184` forwarding, while keeping the productive `direct:160` queue alive.

1. Use the `PURIFIED_NATIVE_UI_ONLY=1` lane as the primary sign-in diagnostic: WebView is suppressed, Roblox native startup still runs cleanly, and any future pixels must come from Roblox-owned GL/UI work rather than the host website bridge.
2. Treat `GuiService::resetGuiStates(DataModel*)` as the current native-login barrier. The accepted UI batch is now proven to be uniquely reset-state work, so the next step is finding which post-reset app/login producer should enqueue actual PlayerApp UI callbacks.
3. Keep the app-only transition switches diagnostic-only. They prove ResumeGame reaches `nativeOnExperienceSessionResume`, but they do not produce sign-in UI by themselves; use them only when probing the post-reset producer path.
4. Continue crossing native Lua/App UI and DataModel notification gates around `LuaAppExperienceController`, `NativeDataModelManager`, `SurfaceController::RenderJob`, `dataModelBindings_onDidLogIn`, and `SingleSurfaceApp::userDidLogin`, with preference for object-shape fixes over broad permanent bypasses.
5. Replace the remaining post-settings bypasses with correctly shaped fake Java flag-provider/settings, lifecycle, telemetry, and native service objects.
6. Model the post-settings config/settings owner currently bypassed at `0x22aaa70` so step 9 can run without the prototype bypass.
7. Expand the render-pump host queue from counters into a shared Java/app task queue with typed callback records.
8. Route the post-settings and render native sequences into a shared host app bridge instead of probe-local latch/dispatch counters.
